src.osir_service.osir_service.orchestration package

Submodules

src.osir_service.osir_service.orchestration.TaskProcessorService module

class src.osir_service.osir_service.orchestration.TaskProcessorService.ExternalProcessor(case_path: str, module_instance: OsirModule, task_id=None, agent_name=None)[source]

Bases: object

Handles the external processing of files based on the configuration of a specific module instance.

__init__(case_path: str, module_instance: OsirModule, task_id=None, agent_name=None) → None[source]

Initializes an ExternalProcessor instance with specified case path and module.

Parameters:

  • case_path (str) – Path where case files are stored and operations are performed.

  • module_instance (OsirModule) – The module instance defining the processing rules.

run_module()[source]

Executes the external tool associated with the module instance.

class src.osir_service.osir_service.orchestration.TaskProcessorService.InternalProcessor(case_path: str, module_instance: OsirModule, task_id=None, agent_name=None)[source]

Bases: object

Manages the execution of internal processing tasks within a Python environment, using predefined module instances.

__init__(case_path: str, module_instance: OsirModule, task_id=None, agent_name=None) → None[source]

Initializes the InternalProcessor with a specific case path and module instance.

Parameters:

  • case_path (str) – The base path where case files are stored and operations are performed.

  • module_instance (BaseModule) – The module instance defining the processing rules and configurations.

load_module()[source]

Dynamically loads a Python module for processing based on the module instance’s specifications.

Returns:

An instance of the PyModule if successfully loaded, None otherwise.

Return type:

PyModule

module_exists()[source]

Checks if the Python module associated with the processor has been successfully loaded.

Returns:

True if the module is loaded, False otherwise.

Return type:

bool

run_module() → bool[source]

Executes the loaded Python module’s processing function if the module is available.

src.osir_service.osir_service.orchestration.TaskService module

class src.osir_service.osir_service.orchestration.TaskService.TaskService[source]

Bases: object

__init__()[source]
static get_queue_name(module: OsirModuleModel)[source]
static get_task_name(module: OsirModuleModel)[source]
static push_task(case_path, module_instance: OsirModuleModel, case_uuid, handler_uuid)[source]

Submits a task for asynchronous execution using Celery, specifying the task’s parameters and execution queue.

Parameters:

  • case_path (str) – The base directory path where the case files are stored and operations will be performed.

  • module_data (bytes) – Serialized data of the module instance to be processed in the task.

  • task (str) – The name of the task function to execute.

  • queue (str) – The name of the Celery queue in which the task should be enqueued.

  • case_uuid (str) – A unique identifier for the case associated with the task.

Environment Variables:

CELERY_BROKER_URL (str): The URL of the Celery message broker. CELERY_RESULT_BACKEND (str): The URL of the backend used to store task results.
